methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Loombotic is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ethan Breit and Lucas Crupi. The valuation universe comprises 9 peers across the sector: 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms, including notable names such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category. A median derived from the full sector peer set would conflate fundamentally different risk profiles and growth stages, making such an analysis unreliable for a seed-stage company.

No triangulated valuation range can be supported with the available data. The absence of a sufficiently dense comparable set at or near Loombotic's stage and scale prevents application of standard multiples-based approaches. Meaningful valuation would require either (1) expansion of the comparable universe to include earlier-stage robotics and adjacent hardware startups, or (2) application of alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis using management projections, or venture method analysis grounded in target exit scenarios and required returns. The key uncertainty is the paucity of public financial data for pre-Series A robotics firms, which limits benchmarking precision.
